name: graphiti-core (Graphiti)
last_major_release: v0.28.2 (graphiti-core Python library, March 2026); MCP Server v1.0 announced November 2025
activity: Highly active: 24.7K GitHub stars (Apr 2026), 2.5K forks, ~148 contributors, 217 open issues, frequent releases (0.28.x series in Feb-Mar 2026). Commits/month consistently high throughout 2025-2026.
storage_backend: Neo4j (primary), FalkorDB (official supported alternative since late 2024), Kuzu, Amazon Neptune. PostgreSQL not officially supported.
representation_primitive: Entity+edge knowledge graph with bi-temporal edge validity windows. Each episode is ingested as unstructured text or JSON, from which EntityNode instances (typed, Pydantic-backed) and typed relationship edges are extracted. Episodes are first-class nodes (:EpisodicNode) linked to the entities they mention. Entity nodes carry: uuid, name, group_id, labels (multi-label), created_at, summary, attributes (domain-specific Pydantic fields), name_embedding. Edge nodes carry: uuid, source/target node refs, fact (natural language), fact_embedding, valid_from, valid_until, group_id, created_at, expired_at.
api_shape: Python library (graphiti-core pip package). First-party MCP server (graphiti MCP server v1.0, Nov 2025) exposing tools: add_episode, search_nodes, search_facts, delete_entity_edge, delete_episode. REST API not first-party. CLI not first-party.
hosting: Self-host only for graphiti-core. Zep Cloud offers a managed SaaS (commercial) layer on top with a free tier. MCP server Docker image available (single-container bundle with FalkorDB Lite option announced Feb 2026).
llm_dependency: LLM required at ingest: entity extraction, edge extraction, deduplication, contradiction resolution all require LLM calls (multiple per episode — can be dozens for a single conversation turn). Retrieval is LLM-free: hybrid vector+BM25+graph traversal runs without any LLM call. Default LLM: OpenAI (gpt-4o-mini for extraction, configurable). Also supports Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini, Groq, Azure OpenAI, and local models via Ollama/OpenAI-compatible endpoints. An option to skip LLM extraction (structured-writes-only via add_triplet) exists but is not the primary path.
schema_extensibility: Prescribed+learned hybrid. Built-in entity types (9 defaults: Preference, Requirement, Procedure, Location, Event, Organization, Document, Topic, Object) are Pydantic models with fixed base fields. Developers pass entity_types: dict[str, type[BaseModel]] and edge_types: dict[str, type[BaseModel]] to add_episode or at Graphiti init. Attributes cannot shadow core EntityNode fields (uuid, name, group_id, labels, created_at, summary, attributes, name_embedding). Structure can also emerge from data without prescribed types (open discovery mode). Schema evolution: adding new attributes to existing types is backward-compatible; existing nodes retain original attributes.
schema_richness: Core entity types: 9 built-in defaults (Preference, Requirement, Procedure, Location, Event, Organization, Document, Topic, Object) plus unlimited custom types via Pydantic. Core edge types: open — edges are typed free-form relationship facts; no fixed vocabulary enforced, but edge_types dict can constrain them. Multi-label: yes (EntityNode.labels is a list). Multi-graph: yes via group_id namespacing. The 9 built-in entity types are well below Gimle §5's target of 20+ entities / 15+ edges, but the open custom-type system can reach that target. The library does not ship domain-specific schemas for software development (no File, Symbol, PR, Issue, Decision built-ins).
temporal_model: Bi-temporal. Every edge carries valid_from and valid_until (world-time validity window) plus created_at and expired_at (system/transaction time). Episodes carry reference_time (world time when the episode occurred) and created_at (ingest time). When new information contradicts an existing fact, the old edge's valid_until is set to the new episode's reference_time rather than being deleted, preserving full history. Queries can therefore ask 'what was true as of date X' vs 'what was ingested as of date Y'.
forgetting_policy: Bi-temporal invalidation (soft-delete via valid_until/expired_at timestamps on edges). Contradicted facts are not deleted but expire. No LRU/LFU eviction. No automatic garbage collection by default. Agent-driven deletion via delete_entity_edge and delete_episode MCP/API methods available.
ingest_latency_class: Real-time incremental in intent (each episode processed immediately, graph updated without batch recompute), but in practice multi-second to minute-range per episode due to multiple LLM calls (entity extraction, deduplication, edge resolution). A single chat message may trigger 5-20+ LLM API calls. Parallelism controlled by SEMAPHORE_LIMIT env var (default 10). Not suitable for sub-second SLA on ingest.
retrieval_modes: Hybrid: (1) semantic vector search (cosine similarity on name_embedding / fact_embedding), (2) keyword/full-text search (BM25-style), (3) graph traversal (breadth-first from matched nodes). All three run in parallel during search(). Graphiti also supports community search (Leiden community detection on the entity graph for broader context) and drift search (for detecting topic shifts). No LLM call during retrieval — purely index-based. SearchConfig allows controlling which modes are active and their weights.
retrieval_agenticity: Primarily static-lookup with hybrid scoring. No built-in Self-RAG or CRAG loop. The search() method returns ranked SearchResults (edges, nodes, episodes, communities) that the calling agent uses directly. Multi-hop via graph traversal is supported as part of the hybrid retrieval but is not a recursive reasoning loop — it is a single BFS expansion step.
retrieval_budget_control: Partial. SearchConfig exposes num_results (max items), and the hybrid search modes can be individually toggled. No explicit token-budget or latency-budget parameter exposed as first-class API. [uncertain]
multi_project_namespace: group_id field on every node and edge provides logical namespace isolation within a single Neo4j database. All Graphiti API calls accept group_id to scope reads and writes. Cross-group queries require direct Cypher. Multiple projects can coexist in one database. No database-per-project isolation built in, though that is achievable at the infrastructure layer.
multi_user_access: Per-agent namespace via group_id. No RBAC or access-control layer built into graphiti-core. Authentication is delegated to the underlying Neo4j instance. Multi-agent teams can share a graph (same group_id) or be isolated (different group_id). FalkorDB backend fork (FalkorDB/graphiti) is specifically designed for multi-agent high-concurrency scenarios.
embedder_options: Built-in adapters: OpenAI (text-embedding-3-small default, 1536 dims), Voyage AI, Google Gemini, Sentence Transformers (all-MiniLM-L6-v2 for local/offline use). Ollama supported via OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Custom embedder via EmbedderClient protocol. Default: OpenAI text-embedding-3-small.
llm_client_options: Built-in: OpenAI (default, gpt-4o-mini), Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini, Groq, Azure OpenAI, Ollama via OpenAIGenericClient (local, 16K token limit, structured output support). LLM can be bypassed for manual triplet writes via add_triplet(), but full episode ingestion pipeline always calls LLM.
cost_privacy: Ingest data is sent to the configured LLM provider (OpenAI by default) — conversation/document text leaves the host unless local models (Ollama) are used. Each episode generates multiple LLM calls; rough estimate for a 500-token chat message: 5-15 LLM API calls, costing ~$0.001-0.01 with gpt-4o-mini at current rates. Retrieval is free (no LLM calls). Privacy: Zep Cloud routes through Zep's infrastructure; self-hosted graphiti-core with Ollama keeps all data on-premises.
code_artifact_linkage: None natively. Graphiti is a general-purpose temporal KG with no first-party code/AST awareness. Linkage to files, symbols, functions, or PRs requires the developer to model these as custom entity types (e.g., FileEntity, SymbolEntity) and ingest via add_episode with structured JSON or add_triplet. No issue-to-symbol chain or diff-awareness is built in.
code_analysis_features: None first-party. No call graph, diff awareness, symbol index, import resolution, or type analysis. All code-understanding capabilities must be built on top by the integrating application.
memory_os_layer: Plaintext (external knowledge store). Operates entirely at the external/retrievable memory tier: structured facts persisted in a graph database, retrieved on demand. No parametric (weight-tuning) or activation (KV-cache) memory layer. No cross-type migration.
self_improvement_mechanism: LLM-reflection at ingest: Graphiti uses the LLM to identify contradictions between new and existing facts and to update/invalidate stale edges. This constitutes passive online learning from new episodes. No explicit RL policy, ACE-GRC loop, or user-feedback mechanism. No mechanism for the agent to reflect on retrieval quality and improve future retrieval.
provenance_standard: Custom internal: every node and edge carries uuid, group_id, created_at, source_description (free-text provenance of the originating episode). No W3C PROV, OpenTelemetry, or MCP-native trace emission. Episode nodes (EpisodicNode) link back to the source text, providing basic audit trail. No structured provenance export.
benchmark_results: Zep paper (arXiv 2501.13956, Jan 2025) reports Zep+Graphiti at 71.2% overall accuracy on LongMemEval with GPT-4o (2.6s latency vs 29s for full-context baseline), +18.5% aggregate improvement over full-context baseline. On LoCoMo, Zep/Graphiti shows strong temporal reasoning gains. A contested GitHub issue (getzep/zep-papers #5) revised the LoCoMo score from a claimed 84% down to ~58.44% under corrected evaluation methodology. No SWE-bench or code-specific benchmark results published.
strengths: 1. Best-in-class temporal model: bi-temporal edge validity with automatic contradiction resolution makes it uniquely suited to tracking changing facts over time — critical for Gimle's requirement to track evolving code decisions and architectural history. 2. Hybrid retrieval without LLM calls: combines vector, BM25, and graph traversal at query time, giving low-latency, high-precision retrieval that scales to large graphs. 3. Open custom entity/edge type system: Pydantic-backed entity types allow arbitrary domain schemas (File, Symbol, PR, Decision, etc.) to be defined, making Graphiti the most schema-flexible OSS option reviewed.
weaknesses: 1. High ingest cost and latency: multiple LLM calls per episode make real-time ingest of large code-change streams expensive and slow; not viable for streaming CI/CD event ingestion at scale. 2. No code-native abstractions: no built-in File/Symbol/AST/PR entity types — Gimle must design and maintain all code-domain schema on top, adding significant implementation burden. 3. Pre-1.0 Python library with API churn: the graphiti-core library is still in 0.x (latest 0.28.2 as of Mar 2026) with breaking changes between minor versions, adding maintenance overhead for downstream consumers.
fit_with_gimle_section_5: Verdict: steal-specific-ideas — temporal model and entity type system are directly adoptable; do not adopt wholesale due to ingest cost and missing code primitives. Ideas to steal: (1) Bi-temporal edge model: adopt valid_from/valid_until + created_at/expired_at on all Gimle relationship edges. This maps directly to §5's bi-temporal axis. (2) EntityNode base + Pydantic overlay pattern: define base graph node (uuid, name, group_id, labels, created_at, summary, embedding) and overlay domain-specific Pydantic models for Project/Module/File/Symbol/UIComponent/APIEndpoint/Model/Decision/Iteration. Protected-field pattern (do not shadow uuid, name, group_id, labels, created_at, summary, attributes) is a useful constraint. (3) group_id namespace isolation: adopt as Gimle's primary multi-project partitioning mechanism. (4) Hybrid retrieval trio: plan for vector + BM25 + graph traversal at retrieval time (not just vector). Code pattern to adopt (Graphiti custom entity type): `class FileEntity(BaseModel): path: str; language: Optional[str] = None; last_commit_sha: Optional[str] = None`. Do NOT adopt: the LLM-at-ingest extraction pipeline for code artifacts (use static analysis instead); the graphiti-core Python library as a direct dependency (API churn risk, pre-1.0).
